that I would test our in market audiences, and these are really just audiences that Google knows are in

to start getting conversions, you need to feed Google 60 to 75 conversions before you can switch over
to target. CPA, target CPA campaigns will allow you to really start to drop the cost per lead, the cost per

So how do you actually optimize YouTube ads? So when we roll something out, we will have a campaign.
So let's just say it's going to be in-market audiences, um, you know, advertising and marketing. And then
we will have ad groups for every single device. So one ad group, we'll be desktop when I grew up will be
mobile, one ad group will be tablet. And essentially all you're doing is you're just, um, if you want to just
target desktop, you would go into the devices and you would set up a negative hundred percent, um,
bid for mobile and tablet and you would do the same for that. And then we allow things to run up to
three X target CPA. So if we want leads for $10, we let the ad group run up to $30 before we make a
decision on whether or not to turn it off.
Speaker 3 (47:37):
The next thing that we're actually going to optimize is the videos. So we optimize for the actual ads.
Second, and generally you're going to have three to five ads in each ad group. Now sometimes Google
will actually lock onto one ad, and if it's not hitting your, your target cost per acquisition, simply turn it
off and it will start to, um, give the budget to the, uh, other videos. Then you want to optimize your
demographics. So we literally go through each ad group and its demographics. So you're going to go
through ad age first and you're going to turn off stuff that's not hitting your target CPA. You're going to
go to your genders, you're going to go to your parental status, you're going to go your household
income and all that. Now to actually bump up budgets on smaller budgets, so you know, 50 to a
thousand dollars a day will go up, you know, 15 to 20% on budget at a time.
Speaker 3 (48:37):
Um, you don't want to go too, too crazy, uh, bumping stuff up cause he could throw the algorithm out of
whack. You don't want to go from 50 to $500, um, you know, something that's kinda getting going. You
know, if you have something that's really worked in the past, you know, you probably could do it if
Google has enough data. But if, if, if it's a newer offer and it's doing well, it's hitting your target CPA, you
probably want to go, you know, 70, 80 bucks, you know, and then bump it up again and bump it up
again. But once we have something working real well, let's say we bumped it all the way up from, you
know, 500 a day to $3,000 a day, we can really start to pump things up. So we'll go up by a thousand,
$2,000 a day and see how the CPA's hold. So, uh, finally, if you have an offer that's working and you
